stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Putin says ‘no point’ meeting Zelenskyy, insists Russia will win the war
stories.summary: Putin’s rejection of Zelenskyy’s offer comes as Russian attacks killed five people in Kherson. What we know on day 1,564 Vladimir Putin has rejected an offer from Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy to hold a face-to-face meeting , insisting instead that Russia will achieve its war goals in Ukraine, including seizing all of the eastern Donbas region. Speaking at a St Petersburg economic forum, the Russian president described an open letter from his Ukrainian counterpart containing the offer as rude. H

stories.title: Kosovo’s path toward NATO and the EU at risk in this Sunday’s elections, ex-president warns
stories.summary: Ahead of this Sunday’s snap parliamentary elections in Kosovo, the country’s ex-president is warning that the results could determine whether Pristina will remain on course to join NATO and the EU. In an interview with POLITICO, Vjosa Osmani took aim at incumbent Prime Minister Albin Kurti, whose reelection she said could jeopardize the country’s trajectory. Osmani served as Kosovo’s president between 2021 and last April, when the national parliament failed to elect her s

stories.title: Orbán’s media slop spread poison beyond Hungary. Luckily, fearless, fact-based reporting endures | Beata Balogová
stories.summary: Across Europe, public service journalism is a shield for democracy. But we have to decide if we want to fight for its survival For 16 years, Viktor Orbán’s government poured millions of euros of public money into thinktanks, institutions and media outlets sympathetic to its illiberal views – not only in Hungary but beyond its borders . In Slovakia, for instance , where a sizeable Hungarian minority lives, Budapest is alleged to have sent millions of euros to favoured media organisations. Many in

stories.title: EU inches toward ‘membership-lite’ plan for candidate countries
stories.summary: TIVAT, Montenegro — EU leaders are seeking consensus on a plan to offer candidate countries some of the economic and political benefits of membership while they navigate the years-long process of joining the bloc. The proposal, drawn up by France and Germany , was presented ahead of a meeting of presidents and prime ministers in Montenegro on Friday. Paris and Berlin suggested offering Western Balkan candidate countries observer status in the EU institutions and limited access to the Single Mark

stories.title: Putin to Zelenskyy: No peace talks — and don’t call me old
stories.summary: KYIV — Vladimir Putin brushed off Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s plea to meet him for talks, as he bashed the Ukrainian president’s manners. “I had a glance at [a letter]. The author of this letter mentions my age. Some of the [world leaders] are older than me. The most important thing is whether you can do your job properly,” said the Russian leader during a discussion at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum on Friday. Zelenskyy sent an open letter to Putin on Thursday, urging him to me

stories.title: Finland: 4 suspects in sabotage of undersea cable link with Estonia
stories.summary: Police say they have four suspects, three facing a travel ban, after investigations into the sabotage of two subsea telecommunication cables in the Baltic Sea late last year. Prosecutors will decide on the next steps.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes cargo ships and admits Romania drone blast
stories.summary: Ukraine says it struck five ships carrying illegal cargo in the Sea of Azov and in coastal waters of Russian-occupied territories.

stories.title: Putin rejects Zelenskyy’s offer to meet and reaffirms Ukraine war aims
stories.summary: Russian president describes Ukrainian counterpart’s letter as rude and says he sees no point in face-to-face talks Vladimir Putin has rejected an offer from Volodymyr Zelenskyy to hold a face-to-face meeting, insisting instead that Russia will achieve its war goals in Ukraine, including seizing all of the eastern Donbas region. Speaking at the St Petersburg economic forum, the Russian president described the open letter from his Ukrainian counterpart containing the offer as rude. He refused to u

stories.title: Friday briefing: How Gaza, Lebanon and Iran have found themselves caught in an escalation without end
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: ​Global powers​ are focused on oil markets and elections​ but those living through conflict in the Middle East feel abandoned Good morning. It’s been another week of brinkmanship via Truth Social and ceasefires broken before they’ve been announced. While US president Donald Trump claims an agreement with Iran could happen soon, for those living in the Middle East it does not feel like peace is anywhere near. People have seen more bombs dropped in Lebanon this week; and the

stories.title: Ukraine: Marine drone explodes in Romanian port
stories.summary: A naval drone exploded in the Romanian port of Constanta on Friday morning, causing no injuries. Ukraine's navy claimed ownership of the device which it said had been "jammed" by Russian electronic warfare.

stories.title: Three jailed over heist of ancient golden helmet from Dutch museum
stories.summary: The men are sentenced to 47 months each for stealing the 2,500-year-old Romanian Coțofenești helmet.

stories.title: Putin says there is 'no point' meeting Zelensky over ending Ukraine war
stories.summary: The Russian president's refusal comes after his Ukrainian counterpart wrote an open letter calling for face-to-face talks.

stories.title: Moment sea drone explodes in Romania's Constanta port
stories.summary: Video shows the moment a sea drone exploded in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta. Authorities said the drone self-destructed after the area was secured and evacuated, and no casualties were reported.

stories.title: Romania: TV reporter flees live on air after drone explosion in Constanța
stories.summary: A live television report from Romania's Black Sea port of Constanța was abruptly interrupted on 5 June after a maritime drone exploded nearby, heightening concerns over the regional impact of the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Felmondott az MTVA vezérigazgatója
stories.summary: Papp Dániel arra hivatkozva adta be a felmondását, hogy az újonnan megválasztott kormány átalakítja a közmédiát. A Telexszel az MTVA azt közölte, hogy más közmédiás vezető nem mondott fel.

stories.title: Nem minden helyi örül neki, hogy Dua Lipa Palermóban házasodik
stories.summary: Az énekesnő a hétvégén tartja az esküvőjét a településen, az itt lakók azt mondják: messze kell parkolniuk a munkahelyüktől, és turisztikai díszletté válik a város.

stories.title: 17 méter mélyről mentett kiscicát a Pest Vármegyei Kutató-Mentő Szolgálat
stories.summary: Azt írták: külső beavatkozás nélkül a cica nem tudott volna kijutni a kút melletti függőleges kimosódásból.

stories.title: Reagált Magyar Péter az MTVA-vezér távozására
stories.summary: „Megkésve, de ma végre távozott az MTVA hírhamisító vezérigazgatója”, írta a Facebook-oldalán.

stories.title: Már milliárdokat kap a Neptun üzemeltetője terméktámogatásra az egyetemektől, de van, ahol nem kérték a díjemelést
stories.summary: A Neptun gyakorlatilag monopolhelyzetben van, így nem is tudtak volna mással olcsóbb szerződést kötni. A BME kicsit több mint egy év alatt egymilliárdot fizet Palkovics László egykori üzlettársa cégének.

stories.title: Ipsos: A magyarok kétharmada szerint jó irányba mennek a dolgok, a választás előtt csak 23 százalékuk vélte így
stories.summary: Azért van olyan téma, amiben a magyarok aggódnak leginkább, ez pedig az egészségügy.

stories.title: Vendégmunkásstop: szigorúbb feltételek mellett, de továbbra is érkezhetnek vendégmunkások Magyarországra az új rendelet szerint
stories.summary: Nincsenek már olyan országok, aminek állampolgárai igényelhetnék a vendégmunkás-tartózkodási engedélyt, a foglalkoztatási célú engedély azonban megmaradt, így továbbra is nyitva áll a kapu a Fülöp-szigeteki munkások előtt.

stories.title: Hivatalosan is megkezdte az előkészületeket Magyarország ahhoz, hogy csatlakozzon az Európai Ügyészséghez
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szerint az ügyészség 2027-ben már megkezdheti a munkát Magyarországon.

stories.title: A migrációs paktum ellen tüntettek Budapesten, Magyar Péter az erkélyről tapsolta meg a résztvevőket
stories.summary: Amikor a fideszes Németh Balázs arról beszélt a videóján, hogy a miniszterelnök hogyan hergeli őket, Magyar Péter éppen mosolyogva szívecskét mutatott nekik.

stories.title: Orbánék bele se kezdtek a fejlesztések háromnegyedébe, a határidő kötött, az uniós milliárdokhoz más megoldás kell
stories.summary: Mire lehet fordítani a közel 6 ezer milliárdos uniós támogatásokat? Ezt vezette le Vitézy Dávid a kormányszóvivői tájékoztatón. Nagyüzem indul: korrupcióellenes törvényjavaslatokat kell elfogadni, és ezzel jön, hogy milyen projektekre fordítsák a pénzt.

stories.title: Rosszul lett az előzenekarként fellépő Loophia énekesnője a Budapest Parkban, az Elefánt koncertje is elmaradt
stories.summary: A főfellépő koncertjét egy későbbi időpontban tartják meg.

stories.title: Varga két góljával verte Finnországot a magyar válogatott
stories.summary: Két magyar kapusról is szólt a 2-1-re végződő barátságos meccs: az utolsó válogatott mérkőzését játszó Szappanosról és az elsőn szereplő Yaakobishviliről.

stories.title: Europe’s far right exploit Henry Nowak murder in UK with populist rhetoric on race
stories.summary: Polish, Spanish and French populists focus on clips of teenager’s dying moments and accuse UK of descending ‘into depths of the earth’ UK politics live – latest updates Polish far-right politicians have claimed that the murder of Henry Nowak symbolises “Britain’s descent into the depths of the earth” as populists from France, Spain and Japan focused on harrowing clips of his dying moments. Despite pleas from Nowak’s family for people not to exploit the killing for political gain and to focus on

stories.title: Trump says Zelenskyy-Putin meeting would be 'great' after Ukraine president's letter - video
stories.summary: Donald Trump says that to end the Ukraine war both sides would have to make compromises, which he took credit for suggesting without explaining further. 'I’m glad that they’re maybe talking about meeting. I think we had a lot to do with it,' Trump told reporters in the Oval Office. Zelenskyy calls for face-to-face negotiations in letter to Putin Continue reading...

stories.title: Nem véletlen a gesztus, Magyar Péternek még szüksége lehet a Mi Hazánkra
stories.summary: A hétfői parlamenti ülésen két meglepő jelenet is volt: a miniszterelnök felajánlotta az egyik vizsgálóbizottság vezetését Toroczkai Lászlónak, a Mi Hazánk két képviselője pedig ellenzéktől szokatlan módon elfogadta a tiszás miniszterek válaszát. Közeledés vagy csak pillanatnyi politikai érdek? Megnéztük, hogy alakul a két párt dinamikája a parlamentben.

stories.title: A csomagautomaták tizede tűnik el a harmadik legnagyobb szereplő kivonulásával
stories.summary: A Sameday, illetve Easybox-automaták leszerelésével és Romániába szállításával megtörhet a dinamikusan fejlődő ágazat bővülése.

stories.title: Hungary introduces stricter rules on employment for guest workers from three countries
stories.summary: Already submitted applications will be considered pending and will be processed, but expedited procedures for work permits will no longer be an option for those from the Philippines, Georgia, and Armenia.

stories.title: A közétkeztetési piac óriása fizethetett kenőpénzt az Őrsiék letartóztatásához vezető korrupciós ügyben
stories.summary: Magyarország egyik legnagyobb közétkeztetési szolgáltatója, a Hungast-csoport tulajdonosa és egyik vezetője is érintett az óbudai korrupciós ügynek nevezett botrányban. A gyanú szerint egy közvetítőn keresztül kenőpénzt adhattak politikusoknak a nekik kedvezőbb szerződések megkötéséért.

stories.title: Ukrán drón robbant fel Románia partjainál, az uniós vezetők Oroszország felelősségét hangsúlyozták
stories.summary: Ursula von der Leyen és Kaja Kallas is az EU szolidaritásáról biztosította Romániát.

stories.title: Tatár Csilla: Amikor 24 évesen képernyőre kerültem, minden arról szólt, hogy nézek ki
stories.summary: A szerkesztő-producer az Alapozó című műsorban beszélt perimenopauzáról, vegánságról és arról is, már másként tekint azokra, akik fogyásra használják az eredetileg cukorbetegek számára kifejlesztett gyógyszereket.

stories.title: The EU should fast-track Ukraine’s membership of the club – it has the most to gain | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Many EU governments oppose it, but the strategic advantages of swift admission could be immense Russia’s war on Ukraine is now in its fifth year and a ceasefire remains elusive. The US’s attention is divided, limiting external pressure for compromise, while Moscow and Kyiv both still believe they can strengthen their respective negotiating positions through battlefield gains. At some point, however, a deal will have to be done. The parameters of that deal are already understood by negotiators on

stories.title: What the Hellenic! Why is Christopher Nolan’s new Greek epic entirely devoid of Greeks?
stories.summary: Set to be this year’s biggest blockbuster, The Odyssey’s cast has been selected to ‘represent the world’. Fair enough – except that one key country seems to have gone completely unrepresented … There are the American accents, gleaming body suits and a muddy Dunkirk palette. And then there is Lupita Nyong’o as Helen of Troy, a casting choice that recently drew racist attacks from the usual moaners of the internet , including Elon Musk, who complained it wasn’t authentic. Authenticity matters. He’

stories.title: Vance leads charge of US officials using Henry Nowak murder to push anti-immigration agenda
stories.summary: Vice-president and state department look to push far-right idea that mass migration is causing civilisational decline How Britain was rocked by Henry Nowak’s murder – and why the US intervened In the state department of past administrations, how to respond to an incendiary event such as the murder of the British student Henry Nowak would have required deliberations, memos and meetings. Given how it has roiled the UK and inflamed tensions over migration and race, the cautious diplomats at Foggy B

stories.title: ‘If it wasn’t Jared, they wouldn’t give a shit.’ Albania PM defends Kushner resort against protestors
stories.summary: TIVAT, Montenegro — Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama has dismissed growing protests against a planned luxury resort as a politically charged campaign fueled by critics of U.S. President Donald Trump, insisting the project poses no risk to flamingos and other wildlife. Albanians took to the streets in recent days to protest plans by Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner to develop a luxury resort on Albania’s unspoiled Sazan Island and the Zvërnec coastline near Vlora. The movement has been dubb

stories.title: In LA mayor’s race, Nithya Raman makes up ground on Spencer Pratt in contest to face Karen Bass – as it happened
stories.summary: LA city council member pulled closer to reality TV villain in ballots counted Friday, now trailing by just 20,672 votes. This blog is now closed. Sign up for the Breaking News US email Nine out of 15 migrants deported from the US to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) in April have returned to their home countries, Congo’s government, a migrant and her lawyer said on Friday. The 15 migrants arrived in Congo on 17 April as part of a bilateral agreement with the Trump administration announced t

stories.title: Trump never wanted to invade Greenland, US envoy to EU says
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump’s threats to invade Greenland earlier this year were misunderstood, the American ambassador to the European Union said Friday. “It got interpreted that we were somehow threatening Greenland’s territorial integrity,” but “the president never said we were going to invade,” Andrew Puzder told attendees at the Brussels Economic Security Forum. During his second term in office, Trump has repeatedly floated the idea of annexing Greenland,

stories.title: EU trade chief calls for new tool to diversify supply away from China
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU trade chief Maroš Šefčovič has called for a new “diversification instrument” to help ensure that industry is not dependent on single sources of supply in sensitive sectors such as chips and rare earths. “Diversification requires a dedicated instrument,” Šefčovič said at the European Policy Center’s Brussels Economic Security Forum on Friday. “The model I have in mind is the Energy Union — a crucial undertaking for Europe,” said the EU trade chief, citing an initiative that he previ

stories.title: Pro-Palestinian activists force EU commissioner to move meeting with arms manufacturer
stories.summary: Belgian local authorities asked the European Commission to move a meeting between defense chief Andrius Kubilius and a French arms manufacturer on Friday due to security concerns. Kubilius had been scheduled to visit a Thales manufacturing site in Herstal, near Liège, together with Walloon Minister-President Adrien Dolimont. But as some pro-Palestinian activists gathered outside the factory, Belgian police claimed it could no longer guarantee the commissioner’s safety, according to local media r

stories.title: EU must prove it is capable and willing to take in new members, leaders say
stories.summary: Von der Leyen tells Balkans summit that bloc needs to make enlargement process ‘faster and more credible’ The EU must prove its willingness and ability to take in new members and speed up its enlargement process, leaders of the bloc have said, as they gathered with their counterparts from six western Balkan countries that hope to join soon. “The European Union has to show that it is capable of enlarging and willing to enlarge, and we want to discuss that here,” Germany’s chancellor, Friedrich Me

stories.title: Israel strikes southern Lebanon after ordering evacuations of nine villages
stories.summary: Thousands flee including from village hosting at least 2,500 displaced people, one day after Hezbollah rejects ceasefire Thousands fled their homes after Israel issued forced evacuation orders for nine villages in southern Lebanon before strikes that killed six people on Friday, a day after the Hezbollah militant group rejected a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon. Hundreds of families left Anqoun, a village hosting at least 2,500 displaced people, after the Israeli military said it

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Attacks on police in Southampton, Russian strikes in Kyiv, the Ebola outbreak and PSG win the Champions League – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Warning: this gallery contains images some readers may find distressing Continue reading...

stories.title: Starmer suggests US ‘trying to interfere in our democracy’ over Nowak claims
stories.summary: Prime minister’s office responds after JD Vance blames British teenager’s death on mass migration How Britain was rocked by Henry Nowak’s murder – and why the US intervened ​Keir Starmer has suggested the US is trying to interfere in British democracy after JD Vance, the US vice-president, blamed the murder of the British teenager Henry Nowak on mass migration. The prime minister’s office responded after the senior Republican politician claimed in a post on X that Nowak would be alive “if the la

stories.title: Texas murder trial under way with 19-year-old accused of stabbing fellow high schooler
stories.summary: Case draws nationwide attention amid debate over racial tensions, as defendant is Black and slain student was white Testimony has been unfolding in the murder trial of a 19-year-old accused of fatally stabbing a fellow high schooler during a track meet in Texas more than a year ago. The case has drawn nationwide attention amid debate over racial tensions, as defendant Karmelo Anthony is Black and slain 17-year-old Austin Metcalf was white. Prosecutors allege Anthony stabbed Metcalf during a Fris

stories.title: California governor’s race: DoJ sends in federal observer over slow count
stories.summary: Experts warn ballot-counting could drag on in primaries for governor, LA mayor and Congress, as Trump claims ‘rigging’ The US justice department on Friday sent a federal prosecutor to observe ballot processing in Los Angeles, as Donald Trump continues to make baseless claims that California Democrats were “rigging” the results to win primary elections in the nation’s biggest blue state. State officials have rejected the allegations, but the delay in results immediately fueled misinformation abou

stories.title: US says Iran radar sites struck and drones intercepted, in latest threat to fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran and the US have exchanged a series of attacks near the strait of Hormuz, imperilling efforts to reach a peace deal The US military said it shot down four Iranian drones that were launched toward the strait of Hormuz and struck coastal surveillance radar sites in response. “The attack drones posed an immediate threat to regional maritime traffic,” US central command (Centcom) said on social media. The military is enforcing a blockade on Iranian ports in response to Tehran’s chokehold on the

stories.title: Xavier Becerra advances in California’s hotly contested governor’s race
stories.summary: Becerra advanced to the general election after emerging from California’s crowded primary field in the race to succeed Gavin Newsom Xavier Becerra has advanced to the November general election in California’s gubernatorial race, cementing a stunning come-from-behind primary victory in one of California’s most turbulent campaign seasons in recent memory. Election officials are continuing to count ballots to determine whether he will face fellow Democrat Tom Steyer, the environmental activist who

stories.title: How disinformation in Congo is worsening Ebola epidemic
stories.summary: The deadly Ebola variant isn't the only thing causing concern for health workers in Congo. Rumors and disinformation are hindering efforts to contain the virus. The patterns are well-known — and could be confronted.

stories.title: Nigeria moves to repatriate 1,000 citizens from South Africa
stories.summary: The governments of Nigeria and Ghana are offering to fly citizens home from South Africa after a spate of anti-migrant attacks.

stories.title: US Senate passes $70 billion funding for ICE, Border Patrol
stories.summary: Senators voted 52-47 to approve funds set to finance the immigration forces for another three years, through the rest of Donald Trump's term. The bill still needs to clear the House; that vote is expected next week.

stories.title: Azerbaijan denies claims of Israel using its soil in operations against Iran
stories.summary: Baku called the reports attributed to anonymous sources and unconfirmed by either Azerbaijan or Israel "completely groundless," telling Euronews it had never provided its territory for any action against a third country, including Iran, with which it shares a 700-kilometre border.

stories.title: Macron invites club of Ukraine allies to attend Bastille Day
stories.summary: PARIS — French President Emmanuel Macron on Friday invited Ukraine’s allies to attend France’s Bastille Day celebrations in Paris on July 14. Speaking on the margins of the EU-Western Balkans summit in Montenegro, Macron said the so-called Coalition of the Willing nations would discuss support for Ukraine and attend France’s annual military parade. Led by France and the U.K., the Coalition of the Willing is a loose alliance of over 30 countries aiming to provide Ukraine with se

stories.title: Experts criticise plan for American-only Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya
stories.summary: Plan departs from policy of bringing CDC staff back to US for treatment and offering support to all health workers Former top US officials and other experts are urging the Trump administration to abandon plans for an Ebola quarantine and treatment centre in Kenya, as the union for workers with the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) calls for Americans exposed to Ebola to be brought home for treatment. Soon after the US revealed it was setting up a field hospital in Kenya for the

stories.title: Australian housing was already cooling before the budget – but how cold it gets depends on two key factors
stories.summary: Most economists believe the chronic undersupply of homes will eventually push prices higher once interest rates ease and the tax changes are priced in Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The government’s property tax changes have become one of the defining political issues of Labor’s second term, drawing fierce criticism from opponents who argue they represent an “assault on aspiration” that will destroy home values. In the three weeks after the negative gearing and capi

stories.title: Scientists make sourdough bread using yeast found in 5,000-year-old mummy
stories.summary: Team now plans to see if they can use yeast strains harvested from Ötzi the Iceman to brew beer too Scientists have baked a sourdough loaf of bread using yeast strains harvested from a 5,000-year-old mummy and now plan to see if they can use them to brew beer too. The yeast came from Ötzi the Iceman, a famous corpse remarkably preserved by being frozen in Alpine ice near the Italy-Austria border until he was discovered in 1991. Ötzi has been the subject of intense study since he was found and ha

stories.title: Outrage in Albania over Kushner-Trump $1.6bn luxury resort – The Latest
stories.summary: Thousands have protested in the streets of the Albanian capital, Tirana, this week against a planned luxury resort backed by Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner. Groundwork has begun on the $1.6bn complex in an area long seen as one of the Mediterranean’s most environmentally sensitive, containing 200 species of birds including flamingos and Dalmatian pelicans. After builders began erecting a concrete-based, barbed wire-topped fence around the site, alarm turned to public outrage at the envi

stories.title: Two men convicted of wounding journalist in London ‘on orders of Iran’
stories.summary: Pouria Zeraati of Iran International TV was stabbed three times outside his London home in attempt to ‘silence’ him Two men have been found guilty of involvement in a targeted knife attack on an Iranian journalist in London said to have been carried out on behalf of the regime in Tehran. Pouria Zeraati, a British journalist of Iranian origin, was working for Iran International, a Farsi-language dissident broadcaster, when he was stabbed in the leg outside his west London home in 2024. Continue r

stories.title: Thames Water should be nationalised, says Andy Burnham
stories.summary: Exclusive: Labour’s Makerfield byelection candidate advocates public ownership of water companies as he prepares for potential leadership bid Thames Water should be nationalised, Andy Burnham has said, revealing public ownership of water companies would “absolutely be an option” under his potential leadership of the Labour party. Burnham, Labour’s candidate in the Makerfield byelection, has previously called for “greater public control” over the companies. In an interview with the Guardian, he h

stories.title: Essex woman jailed for life for poisoning baby son with cocktail of drugs
stories.summary: Emma Barnett killed her one-year-old after a court ruling he be taken away from her A mother who poisoned her one-year-old son with a lethal cocktail of prescription medications added to milk in a baby bottle has been jailed for life for his murder. Emma Barnett, 36, killed her son Oakley before he could be taken into care after a family court hearing ordered that he be removed from her. Continue reading...

stories.title: Man charged with murder of veteran US character actor James Handy
stories.summary: Handy, 81, died after being stabbed, allegedly by Michael Gledhill, whose mother was in relationship with Handy A man has been charged with murder in the stabbing of Jumanji and Top Gun: Maverick actor James Handy, who was in a relationship with the suspect’s mother. Michael Gledhill, 44, was charged after police say officers found the 81-year-old Handy stabbed in the chest and unconscious outside his home in Los Angeles on Wednesday. Handy was taken to the hospital and later pronounced dead. Co

stories.title: Regrettable references and claims of ‘rigged’ election laws: why this week has reignited Jacinta Allan spill rumours
stories.summary: Just months from the Victorian election, the premier’s performance has left some MPs wondering if it’s too late for Labor to change leaders Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jacinta Allan faced three major tests this week. The way she handled them has left some of her colleagues speculating about a possible leadership change just months out from the Victorian election. The first came on Monday, as the premier responded to a parliamentary inquiry that six months ago rec

stories.title: Allegra Spender defends voting against CGT reform – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jane Hume , the deputy opposition leader, accused the government of being “sneaky”, saying discussions around tax reforms in parliament were much too short to properly address so-called “generational” tax reforms. “What the government has done is essentially prevented scrutiny of the changes that this legislation is going to inflict on the Australian economy,” Hume told RN Breakfast. She went on: Apparently, the

stories.title: Kosovo general election: the cost of political polarization
stories.summary: Sunday's election in Kosovo — the third in under 16 months — is marked by a clash between former allies PM Albin Kurti and ex-President Vjosa Osmani, deadlock in the dialogue with Serbia and obstacles to EU integration.

stories.title: Ethiopia: An election without a real challenge?
stories.summary: Ethiopia's ruling Prosperity Party is set for a decisive election win despite security challenges, a divided opposition and some regions not voting.

stories.title: North Korea quietly ramps up its nuclear program
stories.summary: With Washington's attention elsewhere, North Korea has slowly been building up its nuclear weapons program. This week, Kim Jong Un took a publicity tour of a new nuclear fuel facility while promising to build more bombs.

stories.title: Germany's Iran war lessons after 100 days
stories.summary: The war waged by the US and Israel against Iran has now lasted 100 days. In that time, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has drastically changed his stance on the war.

stories.title: EU leaders at Balkan summit optimistic about rapid expansion
stories.summary: German Chancellor Merz has said he wants Western Balkan countries to be better integrated into the EU as they seek full membership. Commission head Von der Leyen called for a "faster" enlargement process.

stories.title: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
stories.summary: Only two survived after trekking more than 50km (30 miles) across the desert to alert the authorities.

stories.title: Hospitality jobs boom as US prepares for World Cup
stories.summary: It is the third month in a row US jobs figures have beaten expectations.

stories.title: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says
stories.summary: Mette-Marit's condition, which stiffens the lungs, making it hard to breathe, has deteriorated, the royal household says.

stories.title: Singer Fally Ipupa's pride at being given major DR Congo honour
stories.summary: He follows the late great Papa Wemba in becoming a knight of the National Order of the Leopard.

stories.title: Everest guide survived six-day ordeal by eating chocolate and 'chewing ice'
stories.summary: Dawa Sherpa was spotted alive by a cleaning crew as he slid slowly down the world's tallest mountain and spoke to the BBC from hospital.

stories.title: Astronauts return to ISS after sheltering during air leak repair attempt
stories.summary: Russian attempt to repair tunnel area sparks safe-haven procedure for five other astronauts onboard.

stories.title: US plans to fight flesh-eating screwworm outbreak with flies and dogs
stories.summary: The insect, which has been found in America for the first time since 1966, is a growing concern in the southern US.

stories.title: EU-Handelkommissar fordert neues Instrument zur Diversifizierung von Lieferketten
stories.summary: BRÜSSEL – EU-Handelskommissar Maroš Šefčovič hat ein neues „Diversifizierungsinstrument“ gefordert, um sicherzustellen, dass die europäische Industrie in sensiblen Sektoren wie Mikrochips und Seltene Erden nicht von einzelnen Bezugsquellen abhängig ist. „Diversifizierung erfordert ein eigenes Instrument“, sagte Šefčovič am Freitag auf dem Brüsseler Forum für wirtschaftliche Sicherheit des European Policy Center. „Das Modell, das mir vorschwebt, ist die Energieunion – ein entscheidendes Unt

stories.title: Protesters clash with police in Albania over Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort – video
stories.summary: Protests in Albania over a proposed luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner , Donald Trump’s son-in-law, are expected to intensify after opponents rejected an offer from the country’s prime minister 'to discuss solutions'. Footage showed police firing water cannon at protesters as thousands of people took to the streets of Tirana for a third day on Wednesday, some of them holding inflatable flamingos to symbolise the environmental damage they fear the project could cause, amid growing calls for it

stories.title: Civilians flee as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire in Mogadishu
stories.summary: Violence flares before protests on Thursday over president’s decision to remain in office after his term expired Fierce clashes have taken place between government troops and militias allied with the opposition in Somalia’s capital, Mogadishu, damaging property and forcing some civilians to flee. In the runup to the fighting, which started on Wednesday afternoon, opposition leaders embedded with militias set up positions in their clan strongholds the city. Continue reading...

stories.title: Jamaican police officer charged with murder after woman shot during protest
stories.summary: Andrew Wilson appears in court over killing of Latoya Bulgin at protest over a police shooting days earlier Authorities in Jamaica have taken the rare step of charging a police officer with murder after he was accused of shooting a 45-year-old woman in a case that prompted violent protests. According to the Independent Commission of Investigations (Indecom), Constable Andrew Wilson appeared in court on Wednesday and was denied bail. Another hearing is scheduled for mid-June. Continue reading...

stories.title: Watch: New footage shows moment Iranian drone struck Kuwait airport
stories.summary: One person was killed and more than 60 injured in the Iranian drone strike on Wednesday.

stories.title: EU considers tightening protections for military-age Ukrainians
stories.summary: EU governments are considering restricting temporary protection for military-age Ukrainian men, as member states debate balancing support, integration pressures and Ukraine's manpower needs.

stories.title: Beijing slams US comments on Tiananmen Square crackdown as 'smear' of China
stories.summary: On 4 June 1989, the Chinese government sent troops and tanks to crush protests calling for political reform in and around the central square in the capital Beijing.

stories.title: UK shoppers return to high street as warm weather brings respite from shadow of war
stories.summary: British Retail Consortium figures show footfall rose in May, with consumer confidence improving after spending squeeze Greater numbers of consumers went shopping last month as spring sunshine brought welcome relief to retailers, which have faced a squeeze on spending since the US-Israel war on Iran. Figures from the British Retail Consortium (BRC) and a separate survey by the accountancy firm BDO showed a bounce-back in footfall during May, reversing a sharp decline in April. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Family values’ African charter condemned by rights groups as regressive and dangerous
stories.summary: Draft treaty claims sexual and reproductive health and rights are an existential threat to the African family An African treaty that rejects longstanding international human rights obligations moved a step closer to becoming policy this week as governments across the continent met in Ghana. The draft African charter on family, sovereignty and values, seen by the Guardian, asserts that African values and culture are under attack from “foreign ideologies” and urges states to withdraw from any agre

stories.title: One Nation, six farcical explanations and no clearer understanding of its housing policy
stories.summary: Hanson’s party is leading in the polls, but it has a long way to go before being recognised as a serious political outfit Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When sent out to do a cleanup job, it usually helps to not make the mess even worse. It took One Nation six separate attempts over nearly 24 hours to clarify the basic details of their policy on foreign ownership of housing. Between Thursday night and Friday afternoon, the story turned from bizarre to farcical, with

stories.title: Dua Lipa and Callum Turner wedding divides Palermo: ‘I could understand if it was for the pope’
stories.summary: While some residents are proud to host celebrations, others lament road closures and city’s transformation into a ‘theme park’ Concetta Chillemi was chatting to friends outside her shop next to Palermo’s gallery for modern art housed in a sublime baroque church in the city’s historic centre. A few metres away, an Italian TV crew had its camera trained on the tiny square in front of the church where event staff in black T-shirts scurried around in the heat. They were preparing for the arrival of

stories.title: UK-EU ‘reset’ summit may still happen next month despite delay speculation
stories.summary: EU’s Maroš Šefčovič says summit will ‘probably’ be in July but sources say it could be put back as talks deadlocked The EU has said Keir Starmer’s upcoming summit “resetting” the UK-Europe relationship may still happen in July, amid growing fears it could be postponed to the autumn as talks over youth mobility remain deadlocked. “The summit is supposed to be mid-July but at the moment it could be put back to after the summer,” said one EU diplomat. Continue reading...

stories.title: What are Europe's alternatives to Instagram, TikTok and X?
stories.summary: As criticism of Instagram, TikTok and X grows in the EU, developers have been working on European social media apps. But can they compete? DW answers the key questions.

stories.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
stories.summary: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

stories.title: India news: Putin hails Delhi ties, Trump calls Modi 'good friend'
stories.summary: Russian leader Vladimir Putin said that India’s cooperation with the US won't harm India-Russia relations, while US President Donald Trump said he has a "good relationship" with Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

stories.title: Australia seizes 100,000 illegal cockroaches in record bug bust
stories.summary: The haul of live insects included dubia cockroaches and Madagascar hissing cockroaches. Authorities said it was the country's biggest-ever seizure of illegal exotic invertebrates.

stories.title: Environment wins of the week
stories.summary: Good news about the state of the planet might seem to be in short supply, but it's out there. Each week, DW's environment desk brings you what went right.

stories.title: Slovenia's political survivor Janez Jansa is back
stories.summary: The new coalition government of Slovenia's veteran leader Janez Jansa is already advancing divisive policies that some warn could deepen polarization.

stories.title: Middle East: UN doubles Lebanon aid appeal amid Israel war
stories.summary: The UN says it is seeking an additional $331.5 million to tackle a worsening humanitarian crisis in Lebanon. Meanwhile, Israel is carrying out fresh strikes on the south of the country. Follow DW for the latest.

stories.title: Germany news: National team won't win World Cup, fans think
stories.summary: According to a poll, most Germans do not believe that Julian Nagelsmann's team will become world champions. Meanwhile, the German foreign minister is visiting Mexico. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Iran at war: Stability masks deepening crises
stories.summary: One hundred days after the start of the Iran war, the Islamic Republic appears stable. But beneath the surface, economic strain, social unrest and growing repression point to deeper instability.

stories.title: Why businesses are offering 'Quiet Hour'
stories.summary: More peace and quiet, less stress — the Quiet Hour is an initiative for people with invisible disabilities. An increasing number of stores and institutions in Germany are launching similar campaigns.

stories.title: Xi Jinping set for North Korea trip on Monday in first visit to Pyongyang in seven years
stories.summary: The trip comes just weeks after Xi separately hosted US President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing.

stories.title: US sanctions Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel as Trump adds pressure to island’s leadership
stories.summary: Thursday’s penalties, which follow Trump signing of an executive order expanding sanctions against the island, freeze individuals’ property and bank accounts in the US.

stories.title: China cracks down on soft porn, violence and materialism in viral micro dramas
stories.summary: Micro dramas have surged in popularity, but drawn criticism for often sensationalist content.

stories.title: Xi Jinping to meet Kim Jong Un in rare visit to North Korea
stories.summary: It is taking place weeks after Xi met the leaders of the US and Russia - two countries that loom large over North Korea's foreign policy.

stories.title: Ex-wife of Dubai ruler's nephew in custody, prosecutors say
stories.summary: Zeynab Javadli had not been in contact with any of her friends since Tuesday.

stories.title: Australian authorities seize more than 100,000 live exotic cockroaches in huge bug bust
stories.summary: The seizure included dubia cockroaches and Madagascar hissing cockroaches, both of which cannot be legally imported into Australia or kept, bred, or sold.

stories.title: Denmark probes death of humpback whale that captivated Germany
stories.summary: Denmark has launched a post-mortem examination on Timmy, a humpback whale whose struggle to return to the sea captured public attention in Germany and beyond. Scientists hope to determine why the animal died weeks after being returned to open waters.

stories.title: Thousands join Jerusalem pride parade under tight security
stories.summary: Thousands joined Jerusalem Pride parade on 4 June, under heavy security, marching for equality and acceptance in a deeply conservative, religiously significant city.

stories.title: Venezuela's Dancing Devils revive centuries-old Corpus Christi rite
stories.summary: Children making their First Communion were accompanied by masked "Dancing Devils" in Tarmas, Venezuela. The centuries-old Corpus Christi celebration symbolises the triumph of good over evil and remains a key cultural tradition.

stories.title: 'Extremely intelligent' bear on the run in Japan after injuring four people
stories.summary: Authorities said that a bear in north-east Japan has eluded capture by opening a window.

stories.title: US actor James Handy stabbed to death, with girlfriend's son arrested
stories.summary: Handy, 81, is known for his roles in films including Jumanji and Top Gun: Maverick.

stories.title: Smart pipelines: Can AI protect the world’s energy lifelines?
stories.summary: As ageing pipelines face growing risks, the energy industry is increasingly turning to AI and smart monitoring systems to improve their safety and efficiency.

stories.title: US journalist pleads guilty to working as China's agent
stories.summary: Thomas Weir Pauken II, 50, knowingly worked with people employed by the Chinese government for years.

stories.title: Fury in France after child murder suspect's criminal record released
stories.summary: A man suspected of murdering an 11-year-old girl had previously been identified as a potential child molester, records show.

stories.title: Five sailors killed as cargo ships struck in Sea of Azov
stories.summary: The cargo vessels Natra and Zirkon — both bearing hallmarks of Russia's shadow fleet and heading to load grain at a port linked to looted Ukrainian supplies — were struck overnight near Taganrog.

stories.title: Lebanon: UN clears debris in Dibbine after Israeli withdrawal
stories.summary: UN peacekeepers and heavy machinery entered the southern Lebanese village of Dibbine on 5 June to clear debris left by recent fighting.

stories.title: Germany: Bavarian lake procession marks centuries-old boat tradition for Corpus Christi
stories.summary: Hundreds of worshippers gathered in Bavaria on 4 June for Seehausen's traditional Corpus Christi lake procession. The event, held annually since 1935, combines religious devotion with local heritage on the waters of Staffelsee.

stories.title: Truck breakdown in Niger strands passengers and leaves 49 dead in Sahara Desert
stories.summary: The 49 victims were buried in mass graves at the scene in what officials called a “particularly delicate and emotionally exhausting task” for the survivors.

stories.title: Fogolycseréről állapodott meg Ukrajna és Oroszország
stories.summary: Oroszország a fogolycsere alatt is támadta Ukrajnát.

stories.title: Szivárog a levegő a Nemzetközi Űrállomásról
stories.summary: A NASA riasztotta űrhajósait, az orosz legénység nekiállt egy nagyobb javításnak. Ezt később felfüggesztették, így az űrhajósok visszamehettek az ISS-re.

stories.title: Tipikus kínai óriásvállalat épít akkuipari üzemet Nyíregyházán
stories.summary: A Sinomatech hátterében egy eredetileg építőanyagokat gyártó kínai állami óriásvállalat áll, amely belépett a felfutó akkumulátorgyártás területére is.

stories.title: Lannert Judit elárulta, miért került be a gyerek szó a minisztériumának nevébe
stories.summary: Ez az ő kérése volt, amikor elvállalta a miniszteri felkérést. A Koragyerekkori Platform országos konferenciáján arról is beszélt, hogy az állam perverz módon sokkal többet költ a felsőoktatásban tanulókra, mint a gyerekek korai életszakaszának támogatására.

stories.title: A CATL Debrecen vállalja a felelősséget a szabálytalanul a csatornába engedett zöld folyadék miatt
stories.summary: A Hajdú-Bihar Vármegyei Kormányhivatal eljárást indított a kínai CATL ellen.

stories.title: Érdekli, mennyit keresnek a hasonló pozícióban dolgozó kollégái? Egy ideig még nem fogja megtudni
stories.summary: Vasárnap lenne a bérek átláthatóvá tételének határideje az EU-ban, de az uniós irányelv átvételéhez itthon még egy ideig várni kell.

stories.title: Nem minden hazugság, ami szembejön veled a neten
stories.summary: Rég volt tapasztalható akkora őrület zenekar körül, mint ami a Geese-zel és frontemberével, Cameron Winterrel történik. Mielőtt a Primavera Sound fesztivál első napját elmosta az eső, 24 óra alatt három koncerten megnéztük, miért számítanak most ők a rockzene megmentőinek.

stories.title: Megvan a megfejtés, kire gondolhatott a betelefonáló, aki 2024-ben a Börtönrock számát küldte Csabának, Zsoltnak, Ferinek, Gergőnek, a másik Csabának, Lacinak és Gergelynek
stories.summary: A Géza nevű betelefonáló másfél éve említett egy bizonyos Gergelyt, akiről akkor még nem lehetett tudni, hogy kicsoda.

stories.title: Putyin nem lát okot arra, hogy személyesen találkozzon Zelenszkijjel
stories.summary: Az orosz elnök Zelenszkij nyílt levelére válaszolt, amiben személyes találkozót javasolt.

stories.title: A Bánki-tóba fulladt egy 15 éves fiú
stories.summary: A rendőrség büntetőeljárás keretében vizsgálja a körülményeket.

stories.title: A Political Capital és Rácz András sem kér a közmédiából, amíg a szervezet élén ugyanazok a vezetők állnak
stories.summary: Az elemzőintézetet és a biztonságpolitikai szakértőt is hívta a közmédia szakértőnek, de visszautasították az ajánlatot.

stories.title: Teljesen megváltozik a mérkőzések előtti ceremónia a labdarúgó-világbajnokságon
stories.summary: A cél az, hogy „összekössék” a pályát és a lelátót, így minden néző egyedi, „360 fokos” élményt kap majd.

stories.title: Flamingós forradalom kezdődött Albániában Trump vejének megaprojektje ellen
stories.summary: Egy egykori szovjet katonai bázisnak otthont adó magányos sziget és egy madárparadicsom került a viták kereszttüzébe. Jared Kushner cége luxusüdülőket és szállodát építene a térségben, és a békülékeny nyilatkozatok ellenére már szögesdróttal, őrző-védőkkel és buldózerekkel jelezték, mennyire hajlandók figyelembe venni a tiltakozó környezetvédők szempontjait.

stories.title: Albán miniszterelnök: Három dolog van, amit nem lehet megjósolni, Isten, a szex és az EU
stories.summary: Edi Ramát arról kérdezték, hogy mikor csatlakozhat országa az EU-hoz.

stories.title: A pszichiáterek beavatták az egészségügyi minisztert a problémáikba, és azonnali intézkedést kértek több területen
stories.summary: Nagyon pozitívan értékelik a pszichiáterek, hogy Hegedűs Zsolt leült velük egyeztetni. Megállapodtak arról, mi az, amiben sürgősen lépni kell, de az még nem tiszta, hogy a reformokat milyen forrásokból fogják megvalósítani.

stories.title: Tüdőtranszplantációs várólistára került a norvég koronahercegné
stories.summary: Mette-Marit állapota jelentősen romlott, orvosai szerint a műtét nélkül nagyjából egy éve lenne hátra.

stories.summary: A színész 72 éves volt.

stories.summary: Biztonsági kockázatnak nyilvánították a kigyúrt, marcona, tetovált férfiakat. Közülük többekről kiderült, hogy Aleksandar Vučić és pártja verőlegényeiként váltak ismertté az elmúlt években. A botrány miatt a szerb elnök került a figyelem középpontjába a pénteki tivati csúcs előtt – amit talán nem is bán.

stories.title: Nauru issues rare statement after whistleblower alleges violent threats against Australia’s non-citizens
stories.summary: Nauran government says its citizens are ‘friendly’ after MP spoke of serious threats allegedly made against former detainees Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Nauruan government has issued a rare statement insisting it is a “friendly” and “welcoming” country after a whistleblower alleged “serious threats of physical violence” were made against a group of non-citizens removed there by the Albanese government. Th

stories.title: Outrage in Argentina after two teen girls murdered as femicide crisis endures
stories.summary: Country is shaken by the brutal murders of two girls, aged 14 and 17, whose bodies were discovered just days apart Argentina has reacted with fury after the bodies of two murdered teenage girls were found just two days apart. The latest killings underscore the South American country’s enduring femicide crisis despite years of feminist campaigning, and have prompted alarm over the decision to cut support for victims of gender-based violence under the far-right administration of Javier Milei. Poli

stories.title: Microsoft to tighten human rights measures after inquiry into Israel’s use of its tech
stories.summary: Announcement seeks to close a difficult chapter for the company after the Guardian revealed its platform was used in mass surveillance of Palestinians Microsoft has said it will tighten human-rights controls when working with national security agencies after an inquiry into how the Israeli military used its cloud technology for the mass surveillance of Palestinians. On Thursday, Microsoft announced the completion of the inquiry and a series of new measures that include changes to how the company

stories.title: EU negotiators reach compromise on air passenger rights
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — After months of wrangling over EU air passenger rights reform, negotiators appear set to leave the most contentious issue — compensation for delays and cancellations — largely unchanged, five people involved in the negotiations told POLITICO. The Cyprus presidency of the Council confirmed that member country ambassadors will meet on Friday at 6 p.m. to discuss the latest package proposal covering all outstanding issues in the reform. The compromise follows marathon negotiations with t

stories.title: German asylum benefits cuts violate EU law, top court rules
stories.summary: In a victory for rejected asylum-seekers, the European Court of Justice has ruled they must be given more than just the bare minimum to survive. But as the EU's new migration pact kicks in, will relief be short-lived?

stories.title: US House approves war powers resolution to rein in Trump's military actions
stories.summary: House lawmakers voted 215-208 on the resolution, with four Republicans joining Democrats, an outcome that saw cheers erupting in the House chamber in what is a clear rebuke of Trump’s war strategy.

stories.title: Albania: Fourth day of unrest as Trump-linked resort sparks protests
stories.summary: On the fourth consecutive day of protests, demonstrators clashed with police in central Tirana on 3 June over a luxury resort project linked to the Trump family.

stories.title: Video shows moment drone hits Kuwait airport terminal
stories.summary: Surveillance footage captured the moment a drone struck a passenger terminal at Kuwait International Airport, killing one person and injuring dozens in the latest escalation involving Iran.

stories.title: Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now,' US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says
stories.summary: The US is reported to have had 17 military facilities and more than 10,000 troops on Greenland at the height of the Cold War.

stories.title: Clash at protest against Kushner linked Albania resort project
stories.summary: Video shows security personnel arguing with demonstrators before pushing one protester to the ground and dragging him away. The incident took place during a rally organised by environmental groups opposing a luxury resort project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: EU backs Pashinyan on the eve of Armenia's defining elections
stories.summary: The EU endorsed Armenia’s PM Nikol Pashinyan and announced a support package to counter Russian sanctions, just days before Armenia’s historic elections. Meanwhile, Pashinyan said he would go to Moscow after the vote and proclaimed that "abandoning Karabakh was my greatest service to Armenia".

stories.title: Dead but deportable: US immigration judge signed order to eject teen murder victim
stories.summary: North Carolina judge said Levi Mendez-Maldonado failed to show up in court – even after being told he had died in 2024 An immigration judge in Charlotte, North Carolina , recently ordered the deportation of a young man who was killed in 2024, citing his failure to appear in court. Judge Amy Lee ordered the removal of Levi Mendez-Maldonado in absentia on 21 May. Mendez-Maldonado, originally from Honduras, came to the United States as an unaccompanied minor at age 17 and was murdered in a shooting

stories.title: Portugal: General strike causes disruption to services
stories.summary: Flights have been canceled and schools closed as unions stage a strike over government labor reform plans. It is the second major walkout in Portugal in six months.

stories.title: Bolivian ministers resign as weeks of protests against economic crisis rock government
stories.summary: The government has refused to rule out declaring a state of emergency and using the military to control the demonstrations.

stories.title: Israel's military campaign in Lebanon threatens to derail US-Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Israel continued to launch attacks at Lebanon, despite US President Donald Trump's heated phone call on Monday in an effort to salvage the peace deal with Iran.

stories.title: Rebel attacks in eastern DRC kill 30 people and hamper Ebola response
stories.summary: Islamic State-linked militia blamed for raids in North Kivu as governor says three patients with disease fled clinics Rebel attacks around a town that is one of the centres of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo have left more than 30 people dead over the past few days, complicating the response to the disease. At least 10 people were massacred in raids on three villages around the city of Beni, in North Kivu, in the early hours of Wednesday morning. Continue reading...

stories.title: A good life for the 99% isn’t a pipe dream: it can be done. Here’s how
stories.summary: Our plan is radical – but by transforming how we live on a finite planet, nearly everyone gains ‘An equal and habitable world is possible’: academics set out sweeping vision for planetary survival Imagine a future in which everyone enjoys high levels of wellbeing; where 90% of the world’s population doubles their income but works half the hours we work today. A world in which the bottom half of humanity sees its share of global wealth rise from just 2% today to 30%; a world where we consume enou

stories.title: Missing Sherpa guide found alive on Everest after funeral rites had begun
stories.summary: Climbing support team rescue Hillary Dawa Sherpa almost a week on from when he was last seen A Nepali guide who was believed to have died on Mount Everest has been found crawling to base camp a week after going missing – and after his funeral rites had begun. Dawa Sherpa, also known as Hillary Dawa Sherpa after the famous climber Edmund Hillary, was last seen on 29 May but did not reach base camp with other climbing groups. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales
stories.summary: Beluga whales, which Marineland threatened to euthanize in 2025, will be moved to sanctuaries in Spain or across US Canada and an embattled marine park have reached a tentative deal on the future of 30 beluga whales , ending a saga that has captivated the public and angered animal rights groups. The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US , ending whale captivity in Canada. Continue readin

stories.title: US imposes new sanctions on Cuban president and Castro family members
stories.summary: US secretary of state Marco Rubio says anyone providing services to listed entities ‘is at risk of sanctions themselves’ The United States has announced fresh economic sanctions on Cuba’s president and some of his immediate family, alongside members of the Castro family, in Washington’s latest ramping up of pressure on its communist-led neighbour. Among those targeted were the son and a grandson of former president Raúl Castro, who no longer holds an official position but remains a key figure on

stories.title: Akár két év börtönbüntetést is kaphatna az a politikus, aki lényegi információt hagy ki a vagyonnyilatkozatából
stories.summary: Korrupcióellenes törvénycsomagot visz a kormány az országgyűlés elé. Az Integritás Hatóság mozgástere jelentősen bővül, gyanús közbeszerzéseknél és rendőrségi eljárásoknál is közbeléphet majd.

stories.title: Az Egyesült Államok újabb szankciókat léptet életbe a kubai vezetés ellen
stories.summary: A szankciók az elnököt és a Castro családot érintik.

stories.title: Megjelent, mennyi támogatást kapnak a parlamentbe jutott pártok
stories.summary: A pártalapítványaikkal együtt a Tiszának és a Fidesznek is milliárdos összeg fölött jutott.

stories.title: Elfogadták az új szerződést, egyelőre nem lesz újabb színészsztrájk Hollywoodban
stories.summary: A megállapodás egyik legfontosabb eleme, hogy szabályozza a mesterséges intelligencia használatát, és védelmet ad a digitálisan létrehozott színészekkel szemben.

stories.title: Megszüntették a nyomozást az újdörögdi kézigránát-baleset ügyében
stories.summary: Az ügyészség szerint a nyomozás során több szabálytalanságot is feltártak, de ezek nem álltak közvetlen összefüggésben a robbanással, ezért büntetőjogi felelősséget nem lehetett megállapítani.

stories.title: Gyömrő adócsalással gyanúsított volt polgármestere is indulna az időközi választáson
stories.summary: Gyenes Leventének ehhez 18 ajánlást kell összeszednie.

stories.title: Megkezdődik a Velencei-tó vízpótlását szolgáló két tározó rekonstrukciója
stories.summary: Már idén nyáron is előfordulhat, hogy a vízmélység egyes területeken 30 centiméter alá csökken.

stories.title: Szabó István szerint az ügynökmúltja meghatározta, hogyan gondolkodik a hatalomról
stories.summary: A 88 éves rendezőt a Ralph Fiennesszal és Viggo Mortensennel készülő, A gyertyák csonkig égnek című film apropóján kérdezték.

stories.title: Tízmilliós értékben raboltak Pokémon-kártyákat egy üzletből Amerikában
stories.summary: Mindössze 1 perc 13 másodperc alatt törték be az üzlet üvegajtaját, majd felmarkoltak több tízezer dollár értékű Pokémon-kártyát és távoztak.

stories.title: Egy videó szerint jóval súlyosabb volt az amerikai repülőgép-hordozón márciusban kitört tűz, mint azt a haditengerészet bevallotta
stories.summary: A tengerészek szerint a hajó tűzoltó rendszere nem működött, így a legénység 30 órán át küzdött a lángokkal.

stories.title: Megint nem szóltak időben Renner Erikának, elnapolták a lúgos orvos feltételes szabadságra helyezésének felülvizsgálatát
stories.summary: A férfi sokadjára indítványozta, hogy hamarabb szabadulhasson. A jogszabály szerint azonban az áldozatok biztonságérzetének növelése miatt a bírónak meg kell hallgatnia az áldozat véleményét is, mielőtt dönt a feltételes szabadságra helyezésről.

stories.title: Nem szorongást keltő, hanem támogató teljesítményértékelést akar a tanároknak az oktatási miniszter
stories.summary: Lannert Judit oktatási miniszter szerint a jelenlegi rendszer túl sok adminisztrációval jár, rengeteg energiát köt le az iskolákban, miközben nem ad valódi szakmai visszajelzést a pedagógusoknak.

stories.title: Óriási felháborodást váltott ki Franciaországban egy 11 éves lány meggyilkolása
stories.summary: A gyanúsítottat korábban már többször is megvádolták kiskorúak szexuális zaklatása és nemi erőszak miatt, de jogi lépések egyszer sem történtek. A kormány vizsgálatot indított.

stories.title: Germany sees record number of discrimination claims in 2025
stories.summary: The federal commissioner for anti-discrimination has said the government's reform plans, the first in 20 years, are inadequate and won't provide the resources and funding needed to combat discrimination.

stories.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
stories.summary: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease, even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

stories.title: 2026 World Cup: 6 things you need to know
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup is to be bigger than all of its predecessors. Whether it will be better is an entirely different question. DW fills you in on what you need to know.

stories.title: Kushner-backed Albania resort sparks protests, EU concerns
stories.summary: Donald Trump's son-in-law is linked to a megaproject on Albania's protected coast that has triggered protests and EU scrutiny over risks to flamingos and endangered habitats.

stories.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
stories.summary: The Iranian-born author of the acclaimed graphic novel and film offered a deeply personal account of Iran's Islamic Revolution.

stories.title: Can Hawaii wean itself of oil imports?
stories.summary: Hawaii imports much of its fuel — and pays the price. From solar to geothermal, the state is searching for a way out of fossil fuel dependence.

stories.title: Germany's UN defeat: What went wrong?
stories.summary: Austria and Portugal have won seats on the UN Security Council, ending Germany's 40-year streak. DW examines the foreign policy questions raised by Berlin's defeat.

stories.title: Germans at Russia's SPIEF: Who's really there?
stories.summary: Russian officials and media claim German businesses are comming back to the St. Petersburg Economic Forum despite the war in Ukraine. But who is actually attending Russia's flagship event this year?

stories.title: Why Yad Vashem is coming to Germany
stories.summary: "Remember" — every child in Israel knows the meaning of this Jewish commandment: Never forget the Holocaust. Now Yad Vashem wants to make sure people in Germany remember it too.

stories.title: EU seeking ways to speed up Western Balkans membership
stories.summary: European Council President Antonio Costa said new ways need to be found to speed up Western Balkans membership in the EU. His comments come ahead of an upcoming summit in Montenegro.

stories.title: Robodogs and helicopters: One Mexican city's approach to World Cup security
stories.summary: In the shadow of cartel violence, BBC’s Will Grant got a first-hand look at security plans in Monterrey.

stories.title: Cuba’s fuel crisis leaves Havana’s streets overflowing with rubbish
stories.summary: Cuba is facing a mounting waste management crisis as fuel shortages continue to disrupt rubbish collection across Havana, the country's capital.

stories.title: France: Protesters rally outside CNews TV over alleged Kremlin influence
stories.summary: France witnessed renewed debate over foreign influence in the media on 3 June as dozens of protesters gathered outside CNews headquarters in Paris to denounce Ksenia Fedorova, the former head of RT France.

stories.title: Ayatollah says Iran delivered 'decisive blow' to 'malicious enemy'
stories.summary: In an unexpected statement on Thursday, Iran’s Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said the “malicious enemy” has been defeated with “a decisive blow” to the US, in a possible signal of preparing the Iranian nation for an impending peace deal.

stories.title: Sherpa guide believed dead on Everest found alive after a week
stories.summary: A Sherpa guide missing on Mount Everest for nearly a week was found alive crawling toward base camp and reunited with his family, who had already begun funeral rituals believing he had died.

stories.title: Watch: Lebanon-Israel ceasefire deal made in 'hope rather than expectation'
stories.summary: BBC correspondent John Sudworth reports from the Beirut stronghold of Hezbollah support, Dahieh.

stories.title: US eyes stake in Azerbaijan's pipelines as economic ties enter new phase after Trump-brokered deal
stories.summary: Washington wants in on Azerbaijan's pipelines and energy infrastructure, a senior US official said in Baku, as the two countries deepen economic ties following Trump's White House peace deal with Armenia.

stories.title: 'Nothing left to chance' for Bayeux Tapestry's journey to London
stories.summary: The 950-year-old work will be as safe "as a baby" during its move to London, French officials say.

stories.title: There is no energy transition — only addition, experts say at Baku Energy Week
stories.summary: Grid infrastructure emerged as the forum's defining bottleneck on the last day of Baku Energy Week, as the IGU's chief of staff said that global consumption of oil, gas, coal and renewables is rising simultaneously, directly challenging the conventional narrative.

stories.title: Sri Lanka care home owner in custody after overnight fire kills 12, officials say
stories.summary: Police said initial witness accounts suggested that the fire originated in a storeroom where dozens of mattresses were stacked.

stories.title: Hezbollah official says group told Lebanon it rejects proposed truce with Israel
stories.summary: A top Iranian general reiterated Tehran's demand for a full ceasefire in Lebanon on Thursday and called for Israel to pull troops back to where they were when the wider war began.

stories.title: UNIFIL peacekeeper killed and two others injured in shelling in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: The UN peacekeeping mission called attacks on peacekeepers 'grave violations of international humanitarian law' and has opened an investigation into the incident. It is unclear where the shelling originated from.

stories.title: Man charged with bringing bomb in his carry-on bag to a California airport
stories.summary: Before the device was removed from the airport, officers put a bomb blast suppression blanket over it and taped off the immediate area, according to the federal complaint.

stories.title: Paris and Berlin push 'gradual integration' plan into EU ahead of Balkans summit
stories.summary: Merz and Macron pitch "building blocks" approach to EU enlargement to revitalise accession talks, ahead of this week's Western Balkans summit.

stories.title: Gaza: Families mourn victims after overnight Israeli strikes that killed at least nine
stories.summary: Families gathered in Gaza City to mourn relatives killed on 4 June after overnight Israeli airstrikes in Gaza City, according to local hospitals.

stories.title: Gaza city residents survey destruction after overnight Israeli airstrike
stories.summary: Gaza residents surveyed extensive damage on 4 June after an overnight Israeli airstrike hit an apartment in the Sheikh Radwan neighbourhood of Gaza City.

stories.title: Watch: Guide stranded on Everest for six days rescued
stories.summary: The BBC's Rajini Vaidyanathan pieces together how events unfolded.

stories.title: 'It looks black' - Americans react to Reflecting Pool's completed paint job
stories.summary: After weeks of renovation, water has begun refilling the historic pool, which has been coated in a colour named "American flag blue".

stories.title: Here's why counting votes in California is taking so long
stories.summary: It could take weeks - or even a month - before we know who won elections in California and Los Angeles.

stories.title: Tough cookies: How pop group Le Sserafim overcame band tensions and internet trolls
stories.summary: The K-pop band say accepting their flaws and embracing humour took them to a new level of success.

stories.title: Mangrove forests are healing after decades of human destruction
stories.summary: Swampy mangrove forests are staging a surprise comeback - which is good news for coastal communities and the climate.
